message.content:
Good - data files already exist from previous runs. The train_full.txt is ready. Let me take a focused approach: write a simple training script that just trains with specific good params and saves the model. I'll skip autotune and data writing since files exist.

{"analysis":"Data files exist. Current model.bin has 0.5734 accuracy. I need to improve to 0.62. My best result so far was 0.6030 with autotune. Let me try a focused approach: train with proven params, quantize, and test. I'll use the existing train_full.txt and test.txt files.","plan":"Run a focused training script that skips data writing (files exist) and trains with specific good params. Try lr=0.5, epoch=100, dim=100, wordNgrams=2 which should give better results with more training.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"cat > /app/finetune.py << 'PYEOF'
import fasttext
import os

print('Training model...', flush=True)
model = fasttext.train_supervised(
    input='/app/train_full.txt',
    lr=0.5,
    epoch=100,
    wordNgrams=2,
    dim=100,
    loss='softmax',
    bucket=2000000,
    minCount=1,
    thread=4,
    verbose=0
)

print('Evaluating...', flush=True)
n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
print(f'Unquant - n={n} p={p:.4f} r={r:.4f}', flush=True)

sz = os.path.getsize('/app/model_best.bin') if os.path.exists('/app/model_best.bin') else 0
model.save_model('/app/model_best.bin')
sz = os.path.getsize('/app/model_best.bin') / (1024*1024)
print(f'Size: {sz:.1f} MB', flush=True)

if sz > 150:
    print('Quantizing...', flush=True)
    model.quantize(input='/app/train_full.txt', retrain=True, epoch=10, lr=0.25, thread=4, dsub=2, qnorm=True)
    model.save_model('/app/model.bin')
    sz2 = os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') / (1024*1024)
    n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.txt')
    print(f'Quant - sz={sz2:.1f}MB p={p:.4f}', flush=True)
else:
    os.rename('/app/model_best.bin', '/app/model.bin')
    print(f'Saved /app/model.bin ({sz:.1f} MB)', flush=True)

print('DONE', flush=True)
PYEOF
